meaning: n.(<<圣经>>中象征邪恶的)海中怪兽,巨物
examples: Democracy survived the Civil War and the developing industrial leviathan and struggled on into the twentieth century.
The simile is there ostensibly to compare Satan with the great sea beast, leviathan.
It is most of all pride that Hobbes wants to tame and of course the very title of his book, Leviathan.
Indeed, even as the dull-witted leviathan sinks, many nimble companies have shown remarkably resiliency.
These allegations concerning the IRS remind us yet again of the inherently destructive nature of leviathan.
